puzzles.themeBlurb: A museum, a Trojan prince, a plaster cast, a Texas town, and a wine upset — every answer carries the same name.

questions.question: Which famous Leonardo da Vinci portrait hangs behind glass in the Louvre?
questions.connection: The Louvre — and the Mona Lisa — live in Paris.

questions.question: In Greek myth, which Trojan prince's abduction of Helen sparked the Trojan War?
What is the common name of the white powder (calcium sulfate hemihydrate) used to make casts and molds?
questions.connection: Plaster of Paris, named for the gypsum quarried under the city.

questions.question: Which Wim Wenders road movie won the Palme d'Or in 1984?
questions.connection: Paris, Texas — a small town sharing the famous name.

questions.question: What is the nickname of the famous 1976 blind wine tasting in which California wines beat the French?
questions.connection: The Judgment of Paris — itself named after the myth.

puzzles.themeBlurb: Ancient wonders, a gunslinger posse, Olympic rugby, a stadium riff, and Shakespeare's ages of man — count them.

questions.question: The Great Pyramid of Giza is the only surviving member of which famous ancient list?
options: The Seven Wonders of the World
accept: seven wonders of the ancient world
questions.connection: The Seven Wonders of the Ancient World.

questions.question: Which 1960 western is a remake of Akira Kurosawa's 'Seven Samurai'?
Which fast-paced rugby format became an Olympic sport at Rio 2016?
questions.connection: Rugby sevens — seven players a side.

questions.question: Which White Stripes song's bass-like guitar riff became a global stadium chant?
options: Fell in Love with a Girl

questions.question: The 'All the world's a stage' speech, describing the seven ages of man, appears in which Shakespeare play?
questions.connection: The speech walks through the seven ages of man.

puzzles.themeBlurb: Every answer traces back to one remarkable year: the Moon landing, Woodstock, Broadway Joe, the birth of the internet, and the Duke's only Oscar.

questions.question: Who was the first person to walk on the Moon?
Which legendary music festival took place on Max Yasgur's dairy farm in upstate New York?
questions.connection: Woodstock ran for three days in August 1969.

questions.question: Which quarterback famously guaranteed — and delivered — a Super Bowl III upset win for the New York Jets?
questions.connection: Super Bowl III was played in January 1969.

questions.question: What was the name of the pioneering network that sent its first message between UCLA and Stanford?
questions.connection: ARPANET's first message was sent on October 29, 1969.

questions.question: Which film earned John Wayne his only Academy Award for Best Actor?

puzzles.themeBlurb: From the Delta to deep space, every answer today wears the same color.

questions.question: Which genre, born in the American Deep South, gave rock and roll its 12-bar structure?
questions.connection: The blues — it's right there in the name.

questions.question: Lake Tana in Ethiopia is the source of which river?
questions.connection: The Blue Nile supplies most of the Nile's water.

questions.question: Which IBM computer defeated world chess champion Garry Kasparov in 1997?
questions.connection: Deep Blue was the first computer to beat a reigning world champion in a match.

questions.question: Which period of Picasso's career (1901–1904) was defined by somber, monochromatic paintings?
questions.connection: Picasso's Blue Period was painted almost entirely in shades of blue.

questions.question: What is the name of the famous 45.5-carat diamond housed at the Smithsonian Institution?
questions.connection: The Hope Diamond is famous for its deep blue color.

puzzles.themeBlurb: Rock royalty, a giant ape, a boy pharaoh, a master of horror, and the world's longest venomous snake — all crowned.

questions.question: Which 'King of Rock and Roll' recorded his first songs at Sun Studio in Memphis?
Which giant ape first climbed the Empire State Building in a 1933 film?
questions.connection: King Kong — the king of Skull Island.

questions.question: Whose nearly intact tomb did archaeologist Howard Carter discover in 1922?
questions.connection: Tutankhamun is better known as King Tut.

questions.question: Which author's first published novel was 'Carrie' in 1974?
questions.connection: Stephen King, the king of horror.

questions.question: 'Ophiophagus hannah' is the scientific name of which animal?
questions.connection: The king cobra is the world's longest venomous snake.

puzzles.themeBlurb: A volcanic ring, a defiant titan, a song of ice and its opposite, an Olympic sprint, and an emperor with a fiddle.

questions.question: What is the name of the horseshoe-shaped belt of volcanoes and earthquakes circling the Pacific Ocean?
In Greek myth, which titan stole from the gods and gave humanity a forbidden gift?
questions.connection: Prometheus stole fire from the gods.

questions.question: 'Game of Thrones' is based on which George R.R. Martin book series?
options: A Song of Ice and Fire
questions.connection: A Song of Ice and Fire.

questions.question: Which film about British runners at the 1924 Paris Olympics won the Academy Award for Best Picture?
options: The Loneliness of the Long Distance Runner

questions.question: Which Roman emperor was in power during the Great Fire of Rome in 64 AD — and allegedly 'fiddled' while the city burned?
questions.connection: Nero and the Great Fire of Rome.

puzzles.themeBlurb: A famous bridge, hopeful prospectors, a Bond villain, a Neil Young classic, and the alchemists' impossible dream — all glittering.

questions.question: Which famous orange-red suspension bridge spans the entrance to San Francisco Bay?
What nickname was given to the prospectors who flooded into California in 1849?
questions.connection: The forty-niners chased the California Gold Rush.

questions.question: Which 1964 James Bond villain plotted to contaminate the US gold reserve at Fort Knox?
Which song became Neil Young's only #1 hit in the United States?
questions.connection: Heart of Gold topped the chart in 1972.

questions.question: What legendary substance did alchemists believe could turn base metals into gold?
questions.connection: The Philosopher's Stone — the dream of making gold.

puzzles.themeBlurb: A falling fruit, a big city, a golden grudge, a Beatles label, and a Super Bowl ad — one fruit runs through it all.

questions.question: According to legend, a falling apple inspired which scientist's theory of gravity?
Which US city is nicknamed 'The Big Apple'?
In Greek myth, which goddess of discord tossed a golden apple inscribed 'to the fairest'?
questions.connection: Eris's golden apple sparked the Judgment of Paris.

questions.question: The Beatles founded which record label in 1968?
Apple's famous '1984' Super Bowl commercial introduced which computer?

puzzles.themeBlurb: On the anniversary of Apollo 11's landing — tides, an Oscar shocker, a sonata, an Eagle, and a Greek goddess, all pulled by the same object.

questions.question: The gravitational pull of which celestial body is the main cause of Earth's ocean tides?
Which film won Best Picture in 2017 after 'La La Land' was announced by mistake?
'Quasi una fantasia,' Beethoven's Piano Sonata No. 14, is far better known by what nickname?
What was the name of the Apollo 11 lunar module that landed on July 20, 1969?
questions.connection: The Eagle landed — on the Moon.

questions.question: Which Greek goddess personified the Moon and drove a chariot across the night sky?

puzzles.themeBlurb: A rusty planet, a Moscow landmark, a WWI ace, a Taylor Swift era, and Hawthorne's famous letter — all the same shade.

questions.question: Which planet is nicknamed 'the Red Planet'?
Which famous square sits beside the Kremlin in Moscow?
What was the nickname of Manfred von Richthofen, WWI's most famous fighter ace?
Which Taylor Swift album, later re-recorded as a 'Taylor's Version,' features 'All Too Well'?
In Nathaniel Hawthorne's novel, what letter is Hester Prynne forced to wear?
questions.connection: The Scarlet Letter — scarlet is red.

puzzles.themeBlurb: It's July 22 — 22/7, Pi Approximation Day. A constant, a castaway, a slice of Americana, a tiny computer, and the Greek who squeezed the circle.

questions.question: What is the name of the mathematical constant that begins 3.14159?
Which Yann Martel novel strands a boy on a lifeboat with a Bengal tiger?
options: The Old Man and the Sea

questions.question: Which 8-minute Don McLean epic mourns 'the day the music died'?
questions.connection: American Pie — pi's homophone earns a slice.

questions.question: What is the name of the credit-card-sized computer built to teach programming?
Which ancient Greek used polygons to calculate pi more precisely than anyone before him?
questions.connection: Archimedes of Syracuse, pi's first great calculator.

puzzles.themeBlurb: An 1883 marvel, The Dude, a card table, a song's turning point, and a London landmark that moved to the desert.

questions.question: Which famous bridge, opened in 1883, connects Brooklyn and Manhattan?
Which actor played 'The Dude' in The Big Lebowski?
Which classic trick-taking card game is played by two partnerships seated North–South and East–West?
In songwriting, what is the contrasting section that connects a verse to the final chorus called?
In 1968, the old London Bridge was sold, dismantled, and rebuilt in which US state?
questions.connection: It stands in Lake Havasu City, Arizona — a relocated bridge.

puzzles.themeBlurb: A desert capital, an Oscar-winning Joker, a secret order, a Mars lander, and a French band — all rising from the same ashes.

questions.question: What is the capital of Arizona?
For which 2019 role did Joaquin Phoenix win the Best Actor Oscar?
questions.connection: Joaquin Phoenix — the surname is the link.

questions.question: Complete the fifth Harry Potter title: 'Harry Potter and the Order of the ___.'
Which 2008 NASA Mars lander confirmed the presence of water ice near the planet's north pole?
Which Grammy-winning French band behind '1901' and 'Lisztomania' shares its name with the mythical firebird?

puzzles.themeBlurb: A pirate captain, a flag, a hand worth 21, a Tennessee distillery, and golf's greatest — same name on every rung.

questions.question: Which eccentric pirate captain does Johnny Depp play in Pirates of the Caribbean?
What is the common name for the national flag of the United Kingdom?
In which casino card game do players try to reach 21 without going over?
Which Tennessee whiskey has been distilled in Lynchburg since the 1800s?
Which golfer holds the record for the most major championship wins, with 18?

puzzles.themeBlurb: A hurricane scale, a Doors closer, a Gulf War operation, Prospero's island, and the cape sailors feared — weather warning on every rung.

questions.question: The Saffir-Simpson scale rates the intensity of which weather events?
Which Doors song, complete with rain and thunder sounds, closes the album 'L.A. Woman'?
What was the code name of the US-led 1991 operation to liberate Kuwait?
Which Shakespeare play opens with a shipwreck conjured by the sorcerer Prospero?
questions.connection: The Tempest — a storm by another name.

questions.question: Before being renamed the Cape of Good Hope, what did Portuguese sailors call the stormy cape?
questions.connection: The Cape of Storms (Cabo das Tormentas).

puzzles.themeBlurb: A tart dessert, a 15-time Grammy winner, Hemingway's island, an anthem's author, and 88 reasons to practice — unlock them all.

questions.question: Which tart citrus dessert is Florida's official state pie?
questions.connection: Key lime pie, named for the Florida Keys.

questions.question: Which 15-time Grammy winner released 'Fallin'' and 'No One'?
Ernest Hemingway spent the 1930s writing in which island city at the end of US Route 1?
questions.connection: Key West, in the Florida Keys.

questions.question: Who wrote the lyrics to 'The Star-Spangled Banner' after watching the bombardment of Fort McHenry?
How many keys are on a standard full-size piano?

puzzles.themeBlurb: Huge planets, a friendly dream-catcher, basalt columns, the biggest tree on Earth, and a helmet catch — everything oversized.

questions.question: What term describes massive planets like Jupiter and Saturn?
Which Roald Dahl book stars a Big Friendly Giant who catches dreams?
Which Northern Ireland landmark is made of about 40,000 interlocking basalt columns?
General Sherman, the largest tree on Earth by volume, belongs to which species?
David Tyree's famous 'helmet catch' helped which team upset the undefeated Patriots in Super Bowl XLII?

puzzles.themeBlurb: A planet, a rock legend, a liquid metal, a winged messenger, and NASA's first astronauts — quicksilver all the way up.

questions.question: Which planet is closest to the Sun?
Who was the legendary frontman of Queen?
Which is the only metal that is liquid at room temperature?
Which Roman god, the messenger with winged sandals, guided travelers and merchants?
What was the name of NASA's first human spaceflight program, which put John Glenn in orbit?

puzzles.themeBlurb: A ballclub, wolf-raised brothers, a two-seat spacecraft, an unlikely comedy duo, and Shakespeare's shipwrecked siblings — doubles everywhere.

questions.question: Which MLB team plays its home games in Minneapolis, Minnesota?
Which twin brothers, raised by a she-wolf, founded Rome according to legend?
Which two-astronaut NASA program bridged the gap between Mercury and Apollo?
In the 1988 comedy 'Twins,' Danny DeVito stars opposite which action icon?
questions.connection: Schwarzenegger and DeVito play unlikely twins.

questions.question: Which Shakespeare comedy follows shipwreck-separated twins Viola and Sebastian?

puzzles.themeBlurb: A Showtime legend, a school in Scotland, the great escape artist, Mozart's final opera, and dueling illusionists — abracadabra.

questions.question: Which Lakers point guard, nicknamed for his dazzling passes, led the 'Showtime' era?
What is the name of the school Harry Potter attends?
questions.connection: Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry — a school of magic.

questions.question: Which escape artist, famous for the Chinese Water Torture Cell, died on Halloween 1926?
questions.connection: Harry Houdini, history's most famous magician.

questions.question: Which 1791 Mozart opera features the Queen of the Night's famous aria?
Which Christopher Nolan film pits rival magicians Angier and Borden against each other?
questions.connection: The Prestige — a film about stage magic.
